original_abstract: Robust multi-object tracking (MOT) in video sequences requires sophisticated modeling of spatial and temporal interactions between targets. In this paper, we introduce TransMOT, a tracking framework that utilizes graph transformers to effectively capture these complex relationships. To manage a large number of targets efficiently, TransMOT represents object trajectories as a set of sparse weighted graphs. Building upon this structural representation, our architecture incorporates a spatial graph transformer encoder, a temporal transformer encoder, and a spatial graph transformer decoder. This design not only improves computational efficiency compared to standard Transformer models but also enhances tracking accuracy by exploiting the inherent spatial structure of the scene. To further optimize performance, we introduce a cascade association framework designed to handle low-score detections and long-term occlusions, which are typically resource-intensive to model directly. We evaluate TransMOT on several prominent benchmarks, including MOT15, MOT16, MOT17, and MOT20. Our method achieves state-of-the-art performance across all datasets, demonstrating its effectiveness and robustness in diverse and crowded environments.
humanized_abstract: Object tracking in video sequences is a fundamental problem that has numerous applications in computer vision and has therefore been studied extensively in the past few decades. An instance of this problem is robust multi-object tracking, which requires a deep understanding of spatial and temporal interactions among moving objects. In this paper, we design a novel tracking framework, called TransMOT, relying on graph transformers to model such complex interactions between objects. To handle the enormous number of objects that appear in a video sequence, we represent object trajectories by a set of sparse weighted graphs. Based on these graphs, our proposed framework is made up of a spatial graph transformer encoder, a temporal transformer encoder and a spatial graph transformer decoder. TransMOT not only runs faster than conventional Transformers but also obtains more accurate tracking results by fully utilizing the spatial arrangement of objects in a scene. In addition, to avoid wasting resources on processing low-score detections and handling long-term occlusions, a cascade association framework is developed to alleviate the computational burden. Finally, experiment results on MOT15, MOT16, MOT17 and MOT20 show that our proposed method is state-of-the-art on all these four benchmarks.

TransMOT not only runs faster than conventional Transformers but also obtains more accurate tracking results by fully utilizing the spatial arrangement of objects in a scene. document.input: Robust multi-object tracking (MOT) in video sequences requires sophisticated modeling of spatial and temporal interactions between targets. In this paper, we introduce TransMOT, a tracking framework that utilizes graph transformers to effectively capture these complex relationships. To manage a large number of targets efficiently, TransMOT represents object trajectories as a set of sparse weighted graphs. Building upon this structural representation, our architecture incorporates a spatial graph transformer encoder, a temporal transformer encoder, and a spatial graph transformer decoder. This design not only improves computational efficiency compared to standard Transformer models but also enhances tracking accuracy by exploiting the inherent spatial structure of the scene. To further optimize performance, we introduce a cascade association framework designed to handle low-score detections and long-term occlusions, which are typically resource-intensive to model directly. We evaluate TransMOT on several prominent benchmarks, including MOT15, MOT16, MOT17, and MOT20. Our method achieves state-of-the-art performance across all datasets, demonstrating its effectiveness and robustness in diverse and crowded environments.
